title: unsafe set_var/remove_var in tests lack SAFETY comments
description: put_with_pinned_version_returns_invalid_uri uses 'unsafe { std::env::set_var(...) }' and 'unsafe { std::env::remove_var(...) }' without a // SAFETY: comment. The safety argument is non-obvious: set_var is unsafe because concurrent env reads from other threads cause UB. The safety here relies on ENV_LOCK serializing this test against from_uri_missing_token (the only other test that reads GCP_ACCESS_TOKEN after passing all pre-token validations). All other from_uri_* tests return before reaching the token read. This reasoning should be in a // SAFETY: comment on each unsafe block so a reader doesn't have to reconstruct it.
